html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twelve — Part Three

The Book of Shemu'el Aleph

SHEMU'EL ALEPH

The First Book of Samuel

Chapters 18 through 24

Yonatan's Covenant — Sha'ul's Pursuit — Dawid Spares Sha'ul

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QSama (4Q51) — Cave 4

4QSamb (4Q52) — Cave 4

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTERS 18 THROUGH 24

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 18 Yonatan's Covenant With Dawid — Sha'ul's Fear and Jealousy — Mikhal Given as Wife

1 As soon as he had finished speaking to Sha'ul, the soul of Yonatan was knit to the soul of Dawid, and Yonatan loved him as his own soul.

2 And Sha'ul took him that day and would not let him return to his father's house.

3 Then Yonatan made a covenant with Dawid, because he loved him as his own soul.

4 And Yonatan stripped himself of the robe that was on him and gave it to Dawid, and his armor, and even his sword and his bow and his belt.

5 And Dawid went out and was successful wherever Sha'ul sent him, so that Sha'ul set him over the men of war. And this was good in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Sha'ul's servants.

6 As they were coming home, when Dawid returned from striking down the Pelishtim, the women came out of all the cities of Yisra'EL, singing and dancing, to meet King Sha'ul, with tambourines, with songs of joy, and with musical instruments.

7 And the women sang to one another as they celebrated, Sha'ul has struck down his thousands, and Dawid his ten thousands.

8 And Sha'ul was very angry, and this saying displeased him. He said, They have ascribed to Dawid ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed thousands, and what more can he have but the kingdom?

9 And Sha'ul eyed Dawid from that day on.

10 The next day a harmful spirit from Creator rushed upon Sha'ul, and he raved within his house while Dawid was playing the lyre, as he did day by day. Sha'ul had his spear in his hand.

11 And Sha'ul hurled the spear, for he thought, I will pin Dawid to the wall. But Dawid evaded him twice.

12 Sha'ul was afraid of Dawid because YHWH was with him but had departed from Sha'ul.

13 So Sha'ul removed him from his presence and made him a commander of a thousand. And he went out and came in before the people.

14 And Dawid had success in all his undertakings, for YHWH was with him.

15 And when Sha'ul saw that he had great success, he stood in fearful awe of him.

16 But all Yisra'EL and Yehudah loved Dawid, for he went out and came in before them.

17 Then Sha'ul said to Dawid, Here is my elder daughter Merav. I will give her to you for a wife. Only be valiant for me and fight YHWH's battles. For Sha'ul thought, Let not my hand be against him, but let the hand of the Pelishtim be against him.

18 And Dawid said to Sha'ul, Who am I, and who are my relatives, my father's clan in Yisra'EL, that I should be son-in-law to the king?

19 But at the time when Merav, Sha'ul's daughter, should have been given to Dawid, she was given to Adriel the Mekholathite for a wife.

20 Now Sha'ul's daughter Mikhal loved Dawid. And they told Sha'ul, and the thing pleased him.

21 Sha'ul thought, Let me give her to him, that she may be a snare for him and that the hand of the Pelishtim may be against him. Therefore Sha'ul said to Dawid a second time, You shall now be my son-in-law.

22 And Sha'ul commanded his servants, Speak to Dawid in private and say, Behold, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now then become the king's son-in-law.

23 And Sha'ul's servants spoke those words in the ears of Dawid. And Dawid said, Does it seem to you a little thing to become the king's son-in-law, since I am a poor man and have no reputation?

24 And the servants of Sha'ul told him, Thus and so did Dawid speak.

25 Then Sha'ul said, Thus shall you say to Dawid, The king desires no bride-price except a hundred foreskins of the Pelishtim, that he may be avenged of the king's enemies. Now Sha'ul planned to make Dawid fall by the hand of the Pelishtim.

26 And when his servants told Dawid these words, it pleased Dawid well to be the king's son-in-law. Before the time had expired,

27 Dawid arose and went, along with his men, and killed two hundred of the Pelishtim. And Dawid brought their foreskins, which were given in full number to the king, that he might become the king's son-in-law. And Sha'ul gave him his daughter Mikhal for a wife.

28 But when Sha'ul saw and knew that YHWH was with Dawid, and that Mikhal, Sha'ul's daughter, loved him,

29 Sha'ul was even more afraid of Dawid. So Sha'ul was Dawid's enemy continually.

30 Then the commanders of the Pelishtim came out to battle, and as often as they came out Dawid had more success than all the servants of Sha'ul, so that his name was highly esteemed.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 18. The covenant between Yonatan and Dawid — verse 3-4 — is the deepest covenant relationship in Shemu'el Aleph. Yonatan strips off his royal robe and armor and gives them to Dawid — the son of the king transferring the symbols of his future kingship to the one YHWH has chosen in his place.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 12 — Sha'ul was afraid of Dawid because YHWH was with him but had departed from Sha'ul — is read as the theological explanation of Sha'ul's entire relationship with Dawid: the fear is not political rivalry but the terror of a man who knows YHWH has left him and watches YHWH's presence with another.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 18 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The women's song — verse 7 — Sha'ul has struck down his thousands and Dawid his ten thousands — is the covenant turning point of the Sha'ul-Dawid relationship. A song of praise becomes the seed of murderous jealousy.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8-9 — what more can he have but the kingdom? And Sha'ul eyed Dawid from that day on. The covenant collapse of Sha'ul's relationship with Dawid begins with a comparison in a song. Sha'ul hears the arithmetic of relative praise and immediately draws the worst possible conclusion. The man who in Chapter 11:13 refused to execute those who despised him now marks for death the man whose only offense was being praised by the women. The covenant sequence: YHWH departed — Sha'ul feared — Sha'ul envied — Sha'ul plotted. The removal of the Spirit produces the progressive moral collapse of the man from whom it departed.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 19 Yonatan Intercedes for Dawid — Sha'ul Throws the Spear Again — Dawid Flees to Shemu'el

1 And Sha'ul spoke to Yonatan his son and to all his servants, that they should kill Dawid. But Yonatan, Sha'ul's son, delighted much in Dawid.

2 And Yonatan told Dawid, Sha'ul my father seeks to kill you. Therefore be on your guard in the morning. Stay in a secret place and hide yourself.

3 And I will go out and stand beside my father in the field where you are, and I will speak to my father about you. And if I learn anything I will tell you.

4 And Yonatan spoke well of Dawid to Sha'ul his father and said to him, Let not the king sin against his servant Dawid, because he has not sinned against you, and because his deeds have brought good to you.

5 For he took his life in his hand and he struck down the Pelishtim, and YHWH worked a great salvation for all Yisra'EL. You saw it, and rejoiced. Why then will you sin against innocent blood by killing Dawid without cause?

6 And Sha'ul listened to the voice of Yonatan. Sha'ul swore, As YHWH lives, he shall not be put to death.

7 And Yonatan called Dawid, and Yonatan reported to him all these things. And Yonatan brought Dawid to Sha'ul, and he was in his presence as before.

8 And there was war again. And Dawid went out and fought with the Pelishtim and struck them with a great blow, so that they fled before him.

9 Then a harmful spirit from YHWH came upon Sha'ul, as he sat in his house with his spear in his hand. And Dawid was playing the lyre.

10 And Sha'ul sought to pin Dawid to the wall with the spear, but he eluded Sha'ul, so that he struck the spear into the wall. And Dawid fled and escaped that night.

11 Sha'ul sent messengers to Dawid's house to watch him, that he might kill him in the morning. But Mikhal, Dawid's wife, told him, If you do not escape with your life tonight, tomorrow you will be killed.

12 So Mikhal let Dawid down through the window, and he fled away and escaped.

13 Mikhal took an image and laid it on the bed and put a pillow of goats' hair at its head and covered it with the clothes.

14 And when Sha'ul sent messengers to take Dawid, she said, He is sick.

15 Then Sha'ul sent the messengers to see Dawid, saying, Bring him up to me in the bed, that I may kill him.

16 And when the messengers came in, behold, the image was in the bed, with the pillow of goats' hair at its head.

17 Sha'ul said to Mikhal, Why have you deceived me thus and let my enemy go, so that he has escaped? And Mikhal answered Sha'ul, He said to me, Let me go. Why should I kill you?

18 Now Dawid fled and escaped, and he came to Shemu'el at Ramah and told him all that Sha'ul had done to him. And he and Shemu'el went and lived at Nayot.

19 And it was told Sha'ul, Behold, Dawid is at Nayot in Ramah.

20 Then Sha'ul sent messengers to take Dawid, and when they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, and Shemu'el standing as head over them, the Spirit of Creator came upon the messengers of Sha'ul, and they also prophesied.

21 When it was told Sha'ul, he sent other messengers, and they also prophesied. And Sha'ul sent messengers again the third time, and they also prophesied.

22 Then he himself went to Ramah and came to the great well that is in Sekhu. And he asked, Where are Shemu'el and Dawid? And one said, Behold, they are at Nayot in Ramah.

23 And he went there to Nayot in Ramah. And the Spirit of Creator came upon him also, and as he went he prophesied until he came to Nayot in Ramah.

24 And he too stripped off his clothes, and he too prophesied before Shemu'el and lay naked all that day and all that night. Therefore they say, Is Sha'ul also among the prophets?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 19. The covenant irony of Chapter 19 is the profound reversal: Sha'ul's messengers who come to kill Dawid are overwhelmed by the Spirit and prophesy instead. Then Sha'ul himself is stripped of his clothes and prophesies all day and night before Shemu'el — the same Shemu'el who declared his kingdom rejected.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Mikhal's protection of Dawid — letting him down through the window, placing the idol in the bed — is read as the covenant parallel to Rakhav letting the spies down through the window in Yehoshua 2. Both women preserve the covenant-chosen man through deception of an authority seeking his death.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 19 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The presence of an idol — terafim — in Dawid and Mikhal's house — verse 13 — is one of the troubling details the text preserves without comment. The covenant heir apparent has household gods in his home.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23-24 — Sha'ul stripped off his clothes and prophesied before Shemu'el and lay naked all that day and all that night. The Spirit of YHWH that Sha'ul received at his anointing — Chapter 10:6 — and that departed at his rejection — Chapter 16:14 — here temporarily overwhelms him again. YHWH uses the prophetic Spirit to protect Dawid. The king who came to kill is rendered helpless before the prophetic community. The question — is Sha'ul also among the prophets? — echoes Chapter 10:11. Both times Sha'ul prophesies against his own character and purpose. The first time it established his kingship. The second time it reveals its end.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 20 Yonatan's Covenant With Dawid — The Arrow Signal — Yonatan and Dawid Weep Together

1 Then Dawid fled from Nayot in Ramah and came and said before Yonatan, What have I done? What is my guilt? And what is my sin before your father, that he seeks my life?

2 And he said to him, Far from it. You shall not die. Behold, my father does nothing either great or small without disclosing it to me. And why should my father hide this from me? It is not so.

3 But Dawid vowed again, saying, Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he thinks, Do not let Yonatan know this, lest he be grieved. But truly, as YHWH lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.

4 Then Yonatan said to Dawid, Whatever you say, I will do for you.

5 Dawid said to Yonatan, Behold, tomorrow is the new moon, and I should not fail to sit at table with the king. But let me go, that I may hide myself in the field till the third day at evening.

6 If your father misses me at all, then say, Dawid earnestly asked leave of me to run to Beit-Lekhem his city, for there is a yearly sacrifice there for all the clan.

7 If he says, Good, it will be well with your servant, but if he is angry, then know that harm is determined by him.

8 Therefore deal kindly with your servant, for you have brought your servant into a covenant of YHWH with you. But if there is guilt in me, kill me yourself, for why should you bring me to your father?

9 And Yonatan said, Far be it from you. If I knew that it was determined by my father that harm should come to you, would I not tell you?

10 Then Dawid said to Yonatan, Who will tell me if your father answers you roughly?

11 And Yonatan said to Dawid, Come, let us go out into the field. So they both went out into the field.

12 And Yonatan said to Dawid, YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, be witness. When I have sounded out my father, about this time tomorrow, or the third day, behold, if he is well disposed toward Dawid, shall I not then send and disclose it to you?

13 But should it please my father to do you harm, YHWH do so to Yonatan and more also if I do not disclose it to you and send you away, that you may go in safety. May YHWH be with you, as he has been with my father.

14 If I am still alive, show me the steadfast love of YHWH, that I may not die.

15 And do not cut off your steadfast love from my house forever, when YHWH cuts off every one of the enemies of Dawid from the face of the earth.

16 And Yonatan made a covenant with the house of Dawid, saying, May YHWH take vengeance on Dawid's enemies.

17 And Yonatan made Dawid swear again by his love for him, for he loved him as he loved his own soul.

18 Then Yonatan said to him, Tomorrow is the new moon, and you will be missed, because your seat will be empty.

19 On the third day go down quickly to the place where you hid yourself when the matter was in hand, and remain beside the stone heap.

20 And I will shoot three arrows to the side of it, as though I shot at a mark.

21 And behold, I will send the boy, saying, Go, find the arrows. If I say to the boy, Look, the arrows are on this side of you, take them — then you are to come, for, as YHWH lives, it is safe for you and there is no danger.

22 But if I say to the youth, Look, the arrows are beyond you — then go, for YHWH has sent you away.

23 And as for the matter of which you and I have spoken, behold, YHWH is between you and me forever.

24 So Dawid hid himself in the field. And when the new moon came, the king sat down to eat food.

25 The king sat on his seat, as at other times, on the seat by the wall. Yonatan sat opposite, and Avner sat by Sha'ul's side, but Dawid's place was empty.

26 Yet Sha'ul did not say anything that day, for he thought, Something has happened to him. He is not clean — surely he is not clean.

27 But on the second day, the day after the new moon, Dawid's place was empty. And Sha'ul said to Yonatan his son, Why has not the son of Yishai come to the meal, either yesterday or today?

28 Yonatan answered Sha'ul, Dawid earnestly asked leave of me to go to Beit-Lekhem.

29 He said, Let me go, for our clan holds a sacrifice in the city, and my brother has commanded me to be there. So now, if I have found favor in your eyes, let me get away and see my brothers. For this reason he has not come to the king's table.

30 Then Sha'ul's anger was kindled against Yonatan, and he said to him, You son of a perverse, rebellious woman, do I not know that you have chosen the son of Yishai to your own shame, and to the shame of your mother's nakedness?

31 For as long as the son of Yishai lives on the earth, neither you nor your kingdom shall be established. Therefore send and bring him to me, for he shall surely die.

32 Then Yonatan answered Sha'ul his father, Why should he be put to death? What has he done?

33 But Sha'ul hurled his spear at him to strike him. So Yonatan knew that his father was determined to put Dawid to death.

34 And Yonatan rose from the table in fierce anger and ate no food the second day of the month, for he was grieved for Dawid, because his father had disgraced him.

35 In the morning Yonatan went out into the field to the appointment with Dawid, and with him a little boy.

36 And he said to his boy, Run and find the arrows that I shoot. As the boy ran, he shot an arrow beyond him.

37 And when the boy came to the place of the arrow that Yonatan had shot, Yonatan called after the boy and said, Is not the arrow beyond you?

38 And Yonatan called after the boy, Hurry. Be quick. Do not stay. So Yonatan's boy gathered up the arrows and came to his master.

39 But the boy knew nothing. Only Yonatan and Dawid knew the matter.

40 And Yonatan gave his weapons to his boy and said to him, Go and carry them to the city.

41 And as soon as the boy had gone, Dawid rose from beside the stone heap and fell on his face to the ground and bowed three times. And they kissed one another and wept with one another, Dawid weeping the most.

42 And Yonatan said to Dawid, Go in peace, because we have sworn both of us in the name of YHWH, saying, YHWH shall be between me and you, and between my offspring and your offspring, forever. And he rose and departed, and Yonatan went into the city.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 20. The covenant between Yonatan and Dawid reaches its most formal expression here — verse 16-17 — where Yonatan makes a covenant with the house of Dawid binding their descendants. The covenant extends beyond their lives and will be fulfilled in Shemu'el Bet 9 when Dawid honors it toward Mefiboshet.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full. Verse 41-42 — they kissed one another and wept with one another — and Yonatan's blessing — go in peace, YHWH shall be between me and you forever — is read as the covenant farewell of two men who know they will not see each other again as free equals.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 20 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The arrow signal — verses 20-22 — is the covenant communication system that Yonatan and Dawid arrange. The innocent boy who carries the arrows knows nothing — verse 39. The covenant secret is held only between those within the covenant.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 30-31 — Sha'ul calls Yonatan the son of a perverse, rebellious woman, and declares that Dawid must die or Yonatan's kingdom will never be established. Sha'ul understands the covenant reality: Dawid is the chosen king. His response to that reality is not submission but murderous resistance. He even hurls his spear at his own son — verse 33 — the same spear he had twice thrown at Dawid. Sha'ul is now a danger to everyone in his covenant family. Verse 42 — YHWH shall be between me and you, and between my offspring and your offspring, forever. The covenant of Yonatan and Dawid outlasts both men. Covenants made before YHWH as witness are not terminated by death.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 21 Dawid at Nov — The Showbread — The Sword of Golyat — Dawid at Gat

1 Then Dawid came to Nov, to Akhimelekh the priest. And Akhimelekh came to meet Dawid, trembling, and said to him, Why are you alone, and no one with you?

2 And Dawid said to Akhimelekh the priest, The king has charged me with a matter and said to me, Let no one know anything of the matter about which I send you, and with which I have charged you. I have made an appointment with the young men for such and such a place.

3 Now then, what do you have on hand? Give me five loaves of bread, or whatever is here.

4 And the priest answered Dawid, I have no common bread on hand, but there is holy bread — if the young men have kept themselves from women.

5 And Dawid answered the priest, Truly women have been kept from us as always when I go on an expedition. The vessels of the young men are holy even when it is an ordinary journey. How much more today will their vessels be holy?

6 So the priest gave him the holy bread, for there was no bread there but the bread of the Presence, which is removed from before YHWH, to be replaced by hot bread on the day it is taken away.

7 Now a certain man of the servants of Sha'ul was there that day, detained before YHWH. His name was Do'eg the Edomite, the chief of Sha'ul's herdsmen.

8 And Dawid said to Akhimelekh, Then have you not here a spear or a sword at hand? For I have brought neither my sword nor my weapons with me, because the king's business required haste.

9 And the priest said, The sword of Golyat the Pelishtim, whom you struck down in the Valley of Elah, behold, it is here wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod. If you will take that, take it, for there is none but that here. And Dawid said, There is none like that — give it to me.

10 And Dawid rose and fled that day from Sha'ul and went to Akhish the king of Gat.

11 And the servants of Akhish said to him, Is not this Dawid the king of the land? Did they not sing to one another of him in dances, Sha'ul has struck down his thousands, and Dawid his ten thousands?

12 And Dawid took these words to heart and was very much afraid of Akhish the king of Gat.

13 So he changed his behavior before them and pretended to be insane in their hands and made marks on the doors of the gate and let his spittle run down his beard.

14 Then Akhish said to his servants, Behold, you see the man is mad. Why then have you brought him to me?

15 Do I lack madmen, that you have brought this fellow to behave as a madman in my presence? Shall this fellow come into my house?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 21. The account of Dawid eating the showbread — the bread of the Presence — is one of the most theologically significant passages in Shemu'el Aleph. The priest gives Dawid holy bread reserved for the priests alone because the need is urgent and Dawid is YHWH's anointed.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full. The sword of Golyat — there is none like that, give it to me — is read as the covenant irony of Dawid's flight: the weapon that established his covenant calling is now the instrument of his survival in exile.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 21 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The presence of Do'eg the Edomite — verse 7 — watching Dawid receive the holy bread and the sword is the covenant shadow over the entire episode. He will report it to Sha'ul with lethal consequences for the priests of Nov in Chapter 22.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — the priest gave him the holy bread — the bread of the Presence, removed from before YHWH. Dawid receives what is reserved for priests because the covenant need overrides the covenant regulation. The priest acts in covenant discernment: the urgency of sheltering YHWH's anointed takes precedence. This passage is cited in the covenant wisdom tradition as the principle that covenant mercy supersedes covenant form. Verse 12-13 — Dawid was very much afraid and pretended to be insane. The covenant hero of Chapter 17 — who declared the battle is YHWH's — is now pretending to be mad to survive among the Pelishtim. The exile does not spare Dawid from humiliation.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 22 The Cave of Adullam — Sha'ul Kills the Priests of Nov — Do'eg the Edomite

1 Dawid departed from there and escaped to the cave of Adullam. And when his brothers and all his father's house heard it, they went down there to him.

2 And everyone who was in distress, and everyone who was in debt, and everyone who was bitter in soul, gathered to him. And he became commander over them. And there were with him about four hundred men.

3 And Dawid went from there to Mitzpeh of Moav. And he said to the king of Moav, Please let my father and my mother stay with you, till I know what Creator will do for me.

4 And he left them with the king of Moav, and they stayed with him all the time that Dawid was in the stronghold.

5 Then the prophet Gad said to Dawid, Do not remain in the stronghold — depart, and go into the land of Yehudah. So Dawid departed and went into the forest of Kheret.

6 Now Sha'ul heard that Dawid was discovered, and the men who were with him. Sha'ul was sitting at Giv'ah under the tamarisk tree on the height with his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing about him.

7 And Sha'ul said to his servants who stood about him, Hear now, people of Binyamin — will the son of Yishai give every one of you fields and vineyards, will he make you all commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds,

8 That all of you have conspired against me? No one discloses to me when my son makes a covenant with the son of Yishai. None of you is sorry for me or discloses to me that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as at this day.

9 Then Do'eg the Edomite, who stood by the servants of Sha'ul, answered and said, I saw the son of Yishai coming to Nov, to Akhimelekh the son of Akhituv,

10 And he inquired of YHWH for him and gave him provisions and gave him the sword of Golyat the Pelishtim.

11 Then the king sent to summon Akhimelekh the priest, the son of Akhituv, and all his father's house, the priests who were at Nov, and all of them came to the king.

12 And Sha'ul said, Hear now, son of Akhituv. And he answered, Here I am, my lord.

13 And Sha'ul said to him, Why have you conspired against me, you and the son of Yishai, in that you have given him bread and a sword and have inquired of Creator for him, so that he has risen against me, to lie in wait, as at this day?

14 Then Akhimelekh answered the king, And who among all your servants is so faithful as Dawid, who is the king's son-in-law, and captain over your bodyguard, and honored in your house?

15 Is today the first time that I have inquired of Creator for him? No. Let not the king impute anything to his servant or to all the house of my father, for your servant has known nothing of all this, much or little.

16 And the king said, You shall surely die, Akhimelekh, you and all your father's house.

17 And the king said to the guard who stood about him, Turn and kill the priests of YHWH, because their hand also is with Dawid, and they knew that he fled and did not disclose it to me. But the servants of the king would not put out their hand to strike the priests of YHWH.

18 Then the king said to Do'eg, You turn and strike the priests. And Do'eg the Edomite turned and struck the priests, and he killed on that day eighty-five persons who wore the linen ephod.

19 And Nov, the city of the priests, he put to the sword — both man and woman, child and infant, ox, donkey, and sheep, he put to the sword.

20 But one of the sons of Akhimelekh the son of Akhituv, named Evyatar, escaped and fled after Dawid.

21 And Evyatar told Dawid that Sha'ul had killed the priests of YHWH.

22 And Dawid said to Evyatar, I knew on that day, when Do'eg the Edomite was there, that he would surely tell Sha'ul. I have occasioned the death of all the persons of your father's house.

23 Stay with me — do not be afraid, for he who seeks my life seeks your life. With me you shall be in safekeeping.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 22. The massacre of the priests of Nov — eighty-five priests killed by Do'eg the Edomite — is the covenant catastrophe of Sha'ul's final spiral. The king who refused to execute the Amalekites as YHWH commanded now executes YHWH's own priests at the word of an Edomite.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Dawid's gathering at the cave of Adullam — verse 1-2 — every man in distress, in debt, and bitter in soul — is read as the covenant community of the rejected gathering around the rejected anointed one: a community of the marginalized becoming the core of the future kingdom.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 22 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The covenant irony of verse 17 — the king's servants refused to strike the priests of YHWH — even Sha'ul's own bodyguard would not execute YHWH's priests. Only Do'eg the Edomite, who had no covenant obligation to YHWH's priests, was willing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18-19 — Do'eg struck the priests and killed eighty-five persons who wore the linen ephod. And Nov, the city of the priests, he put to the sword — man, woman, child, infant, ox, donkey, sheep. The same language as the herem commanded against Amalek in Chapter 15 — which Sha'ul refused to carry out — is now applied to the priests of YHWH. Sha'ul performs against the covenant community what he refused to perform against the covenant enemy. Verse 22 — Dawid said to Evyatar, I knew on that day — I have occasioned the death of all the persons of your father's house. Dawid accepts the covenant responsibility for the consequence of his choices without deflection. The same moral clarity that will mark his kingship is visible here in his darkest exile moment.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 23 Dawid Saves Ke'ilah — Yonatan Strengthens Dawid's Hand — The Ziph Betrayal

1 Now they told Dawid, Behold, the Pelishtim are fighting against Ke'ilah and are robbing the threshing floors.

2 Therefore Dawid inquired of YHWH, Shall I go and attack these Pelishtim? And YHWH said to Dawid, Go and attack the Pelishtim and save Ke'ilah.

3 But Dawid's men said to him, Behold, we are afraid here in Yehudah. How much more then if we go to Ke'ilah against the armies of the Pelishtim?

4 Then Dawid inquired of YHWH again. And YHWH answered him, Arise, go down to Ke'ilah, for I will give the Pelishtim into your hand.

5 And Dawid and his men went to Ke'ilah and fought with the Pelishtim and brought away their livestock and struck them with a great blow. So Dawid saved the inhabitants of Ke'ilah.

6 When Evyatar the son of Akhimelekh had fled to Dawid to Ke'ilah, he had come down with an ephod in his hand.

7 Now it was told Sha'ul that Dawid had come to Ke'ilah. And Sha'ul said, Creator has given him into my hand, for he has shut himself in by entering a town that has gates and bars.

8 And Sha'ul summoned all the people to war, to go down to Ke'ilah, to besiege Dawid and his men.

9 Dawid knew that Sha'ul was plotting harm against him. And he said to Evyatar the priest, Bring the ephod here.

10 Then Dawid said, O YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, your servant has surely heard that Sha'ul seeks to come to Ke'ilah, to destroy the city on my account.

11 Will the men of Ke'ilah surrender me into his hand? Will Sha'ul come down, as your servant has heard? O YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, please tell your servant. And YHWH said, He will come down.

12 Then Dawid said, Will the men of Ke'ilah surrender me and my men into the hand of Sha'ul? And YHWH said, They will surrender you.

13 Then Dawid and his men, who were about six hundred, arose and departed from Ke'ilah, and they went wherever they could go. When Sha'ul was told that Dawid had escaped from Ke'ilah, he gave up the expedition.

14 And Dawid remained in the strongholds in the wilderness, in the hill country of the wilderness of Ziph. And Sha'ul sought him every day, but Creator did not give him into his hand.

15 And Dawid saw that Sha'ul had come out to seek his life. Dawid was in the wilderness of Ziph at Khoresh.

16 And Yonatan, Sha'ul's son, rose and went to Dawid at Khoresh, and strengthened his hand in Creator.

17 And he said to him, Do not fear, for the hand of Sha'ul my father shall not find you. You shall be king over Yisra'EL, and I shall be next to you. Sha'ul my father also knows this.

18 And the two of them made a covenant before YHWH. Dawid remained at Khoresh, and Yonatan went home.

19 Then the Ziphites went up to Sha'ul at Giv'ah, saying, Is not Dawid hiding among us in the strongholds at Khoresh, on the hill of Khakhilah, which is south of Yeshimon?

20 Now come down, O king, according to all your heart's desire to come down, and our part shall be to surrender him into the king's hand.

21 And Sha'ul said, May you be blessed by YHWH, for you have had compassion on me.

22 Go, make yet more sure. Know and see the place where his foot is, and who has seen him there, for it is told me that he is very cunning.

23 See therefore, and take note of all the lurking places where he hides, and come back to me with sure information. Then I will go with you. And if he is in the land, I will search him out among all the thousands of Yehudah.

24 And they arose and went to Ziph ahead of Sha'ul. But Dawid and his men were in the wilderness of Ma'on, in the Aravah to the south of Yeshimon.

25 And Sha'ul and his men went to seek him. And Dawid was told, so he went down to the rock and lived in the wilderness of Ma'on. When Sha'ul heard that, he pursued after Dawid in the wilderness of Ma'on.

26 Sha'ul went on one side of the mountain, and Dawid and his men on the other side of the mountain. And Dawid was hurrying to get away from Sha'ul. As Sha'ul and his men were closing in on Dawid and his men to capture them,

27 A messenger came to Sha'ul, saying, Hurry and come, for the Pelishtim have made a raid against the land.

28 So Sha'ul returned from pursuing after Dawid and went against the Pelishtim. Therefore that place was called the Rock of Escape.

29 And Dawid went up from there and lived in the strongholds of En-Gedi.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 23. Verse 16 — Yonatan rose and went to Dawid at Khoresh and strengthened his hand in Creator — is one of the most significant covenant acts in the entire Shemu'el narrative. The son of the king who is hunting Dawid risks his life to come to the fugitive and deliver the covenant word of assurance.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Dawid's sustained use of the ephod and Urim to inquire of YHWH throughout the wilderness period is read as the covenant discipline of the fugitive: even in flight, the covenant leader seeks YHWH's direction before every major decision.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 23 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The people of Ke'ilah — the city Dawid saved — were prepared to hand him over to Sha'ul — verse 12. YHWH's answer to the inquiry is honest and devastating: they will surrender you. Dawid acts on the hard word and escapes.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16-17 — Yonatan strengthened his hand in Creator — do not fear — you shall be king over Yisra'EL and I shall be next to you. Yonatan knows he will not be king. His father knows it — verse 17. Yonatan comes to Dawid in the wilderness not to claim anything but to give the covenant word that Dawid needs. He strengthened his hand in Creator — the act is entirely for Dawid's benefit. The last recorded meeting between Yonatan and Dawid is this: the prince who will not be king strengthening the shepherd who will be. Verse 28 — the Rock of Escape. The Pelishtim raid that interrupted Sha'ul's pursuit of Dawid when he was moments from capture is covenant providence: YHWH uses the enemies of the covenant people to protect his anointed.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 24 Dawid Spares Sha'ul in the Cave — I Will Not Put Out My Hand Against YHWH's Anointed — YHWH Judge Between Us

1 When Sha'ul returned from following the Pelishtim, he was told, Behold, Dawid is in the wilderness of En-Gedi.

2 Then Sha'ul took three thousand chosen men out of all Yisra'EL and went to seek Dawid and his men in front of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.

3 And he came to the sheepfolds by the way, where there was a cave, and Sha'ul went in to relieve himself. Now Dawid and his men were sitting in the innermost parts of the cave.

4 And the men of Dawid said to him, Here is the day of which YHWH said to you, Behold, I will give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it shall seem good to you. Then Dawid arose and stealthily cut off a corner of Sha'ul's robe.

5 And afterward Dawid's heart struck him, because he had cut off a corner of Sha'ul's robe.

6 He said to his men, YHWH forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, YHWH's anointed, to put out my hand against him, seeing he is YHWH's anointed.

7 So Dawid persuaded his men with these words and did not permit them to attack Sha'ul. And Sha'ul rose up and left the cave and went on his way.

8 Afterward Dawid also arose and went out of the cave, and called after Sha'ul, My lord the king. And when Sha'ul looked behind him, Dawid bowed with his face to the earth and paid homage.

9 And Dawid said to Sha'ul, Why do you listen to the words of men who say, Behold, Dawid seeks your harm?

10 Behold, this day your eyes have seen how YHWH gave you today into my hand in the cave. And some told me to kill you, but I spared you. I said, I will not put out my hand against my lord, for he is YHWH's anointed.

11 See, my father, see the corner of your robe in my hand. For by the fact that I cut off the corner of your robe and did not kill you, you may know and see that there is no wrong or treason in my hand. I have not sinned against you, though you hunt my life to take it.

12 May YHWH judge between me and you, may YHWH avenge me against you, but my hand shall not be against you.

13 As the proverb of the ancients says, Out of the wicked comes wickedness. But my hand shall not be against you.

14 After whom has the king of Yisra'EL come out? After whom do you pursue? After a dead dog. After a flea.

15 May YHWH therefore be judge and give sentence between me and you, and see to it and plead my cause and deliver me from your hand.

16 As soon as Dawid had finished speaking these words to Sha'ul, Sha'ul said, Is this your voice, my son Dawid? And Sha'ul lifted up his voice and wept.

17 He said to Dawid, You are more righteous than I, for you have repaid me good, whereas I have repaid you evil.

18 And you have declared this day how you have dealt well with me, in that you did not kill me when YHWH put me into your hands.

19 For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him go away safe? So may YHWH reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.

20 And now, behold, I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Yisra'EL shall be established in your hand.

21 Swear to me therefore by YHWH that you will not cut off my offspring after me, and that you will not destroy my name out of my father's house.

22 And Dawid swore this to Sha'ul. Then Sha'ul went home, but Dawid and his men went up to the stronghold.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 24. The cave of En-Gedi is the covenant test of Dawid's character at its most concentrated. YHWH delivers Sha'ul into Dawid's hand. Dawid's men declare it the day YHWH promised. Dawid cuts the corner of the robe — then his heart strikes him.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 6 — YHWH forbid that I should put out my hand against him, seeing he is YHWH's anointed — is read as the covenant principle that governs Dawid's entire relationship with Sha'ul: the anointing is from YHWH, and only YHWH can remove it. Dawid will not take by force what YHWH must give.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 24 largely consistent with the Masoretic. Verse 5 — Dawid's heart struck him because he cut the corner of Sha'ul's robe — is one of the most theologically precise verses in the narrative. Dawid is troubled not by what he failed to do but by what he did do, because the robe itself was the symbol of Sha'ul's covenant investiture.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — YHWH forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, YHWH's anointed. Dawid's covenant restraint is not political calculation — it is covenant theology. He has every justification, every opportunity, every encouragement from his own men. He refuses not because killing Sha'ul would be strategically unwise but because Sha'ul is YHWH's anointed. The anointing cannot be violated by the one who also carries an anointing. Verse 17 — you are more righteous than I, for you have repaid me good whereas I have repaid you evil. Sha'ul's acknowledgment in this moment is complete and genuine. And it will not last. The pattern of Sha'ul's repentance — real in the moment, unfulfilled in the days that follow — is the covenant tragedy of a man whose insight exceeds his will.

END OF PART THREE — CHAPTERS 18 THROUGH 24
